content:
# Mark Chapter 11 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 38r–38v (PDF pages 75–76) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_011_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_011_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And when they-draw-near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he-sends two of his disciples

² and says to-them, "Go into the village opposite you, and immediately entering into it you-will-find a-colt tied, upon which no-one of-men has-yet sat. Loose it and bring [it].

³ And if anyone says to-you, 'Why are-you-doing this?' say, 'The Lord has need of-it, and immediately he-sends it back here.'"

⁴ And they-went-away and found a-colt tied at a-door outside on the street, and they-loose it.

⁵ And some of-those standing there were-saying to-them, "What are-you-doing, loosing the colt?"

⁶ And they-spoke to-them just-as Jesus said, and they-let them-[go].

⁷ And they-bring the colt to Jesus and throw-on it their garments, and he-sat upon it.

⁸ And many spread their garments on the road, and others [spread] leafy-branches, having-cut [them] from the fields.

⁹ And those going-before and those following were-crying-out, "Hosanna! Blessed [is] the-one coming in [the] name of-[the] Lord!

¹⁰ Blessed [is] the coming kingdom of-our father David! Hosanna in the highest!"

¹¹ And he-entered into Jerusalem, into the temple. And having-looked-around [at] all-things, the hour already being late, he-went-out to Bethany with the twelve.

¹² And on-the-next-day, having-gone-out from Bethany, he-hungered.

¹³ And having-seen a-fig-tree from-afar having leaves, he-came [to see] if perhaps he-will-find something on it. And having-come to it, he-found nothing except leaves, for it-was not the season of-figs.

¹⁴ And answering, he-said to-it, "May no-one eat fruit from you any-longer forever." And his disciples were-hearing.

¹⁵ And they-come to Jerusalem. And having-entered into the temple, he-began to-cast-out those selling and those buying in the temple, and the tables of-the money-changers and the seats of-those selling the doves he-overturned.

¹⁶ And he-was not permitting that anyone should-carry a-vessel through the temple.

¹⁷ And he-was-teaching and was-saying to-them, "Has-it not been-written, 'My house will-be-called a-house of-prayer for-all the nations'? But you have-made it a-den of-robbers."

¹⁸ And the chief-priests and the scribes heard, and they-were-seeking how they-might-destroy him; for they-were-fearing him, because all the crowd was-being-astonished at his teaching.

¹⁹ And when it-became late, they-were-going-out outside the city.

²⁰ And passing-by early, they-saw the fig-tree having-been-dried from [the] roots.

²¹ And Peter, having-remembered, says to-him, "Rabbi, look! The fig-tree which you-cursed has-been-dried-up."

²² And answering, Jesus says to-them, "Have faith of-God.

²³ Truly I-say to-you that whoever says to-this mountain, 'Be-taken-up and be-thrown into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he-says happens, it-will-be to-him.

²⁴ For-this-reason I-say to-you, all-things whatever you-pray and ask, believe that you-received, and it-will-be to-you.

²⁵ And when you-stand praying, forgive if you-have anything against anyone, so-that your Father who [is] in the heavens might-also-forgive you your trespasses."

²⁶ [But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father who is in the heavens forgive your trespasses.]

²⁷ And they-come again to Jerusalem. And as-he-was-walking in the temple, the chief-priests and the scribes and the elders come to-him,

²⁸ and they-were-saying to-him, "By what authority do-you-do these-things? Or who gave you this authority to-do these-things?"

²⁹ And Jesus said to-them, "I-will-ask you one word, and answer me, and I-will-tell you by what authority I-do these-things.

³⁰ The baptism of John — was it from heaven or from men? Answer me."

³¹ And they-were-reasoning among themselves, saying, "If we-say, 'From heaven,' he-will-say, 'Why then did-you not believe him?'

³² But if we-say, 'From men'..." — they-were-fearing the crowd, for all held that John truly was a-prophet.

³³ And answering Jesus, they-say, "We-do not know." And Jesus says to-them, "Neither do-I tell you by what authority I-do these-things."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **11:10 — "the coming kingdom of our father David":** This acclamation is unique to Mark. Matthew and Luke modify it. Sinaiticus preserves it clearly.

2. **11:13 — "for it was not the season of figs":** Mark's editorial comment creates a theological puzzle — why curse a tree for having no fruit when it's not fig season? The episode functions symbolically: the fig tree represents the temple, full of leaves (religious activity) but barren of fruit.

3. **11:17 — Combined quotation:** Isaiah 56:7 ("house of prayer for all nations") and Jeremiah 7:11 ("den of robbers"). The phrase "for all the nations" is significant — Jesus protests the commercialization of the court of the Gentiles, the one space in the temple open to non-Jews.

4. **11:22 — "faith of God" (ΠΙΣΤΙΝ ΘΕΟΥ):** The genitive is ambiguous — it could mean "faith in God" (objective genitive) or "God's own faithfulness" (subjective genitive). I preserve the literal construction.

5. **11:26 — Bracketed verse:** Absent from Sinaiticus and Vaticanus. It is a scribal addition from Matthew 6:15. I bracket it as textually secondary.

— EMET, AI Translator
